question: Which vision was recently launched by India as part of its campaign for the 2028–29 UN Security Council (UNSC) Non-Permanent Membership?
detail:
- India launched the **'SHANTI Vision'** as part of its campaign for a **Non-Permanent Member seat in the UN Security Council (UNSC) for 2028–29**.
- The campaign was launched by **External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar** at the **United Nations (UN) headquarters, New York**.
- The Vision aims to strengthen a **Rules-based Global Order**, **International Norms**, **Mutual Trust** and the voice of the **Global South** in global decision-making.
- **SHANTI Full Form:** **S – Securing, H – Holistic, A – Advancement, N – through Norms, T – Trust, I – Integrity**.
- **What India wants:** a Non-Permanent Member seat for the **2028–29 term**, giving it the right to vote and take part in decisions on global peace & security; to raise its voice firmly on **Terrorism, Maritime Security and Climate Change**; to better represent the **Global South (developing countries)**; to increase India's diplomatic and global influence; and to push its demand for **UNSC Reforms and Permanent Membership**.
- **About UNSC:** established **1945**; total members **15 (5 Permanent + 10 Non-Permanent)**; Non-Permanent Members are elected for **2 years** by a **2/3 majority in the UN General Assembly**.
- **India & UNSC:** India has been a Non-Permanent Member **8 times** — **1950–51, 1967–68, 1972–73, 1977–78, 1984–85, 1991–92, 2011–12, 2021–22**.

question: The annual Jagannath Rath Yatra is celebrated at which place?
detail:
- The annual **Jagannath Rath Yatra** is held at **Puri, Odisha**.
- This year the Rath Yatra began on **16 July** in Odisha and will conclude on **24 July**.
- The Yatra begins with the **Deba Snana Jatra (Snana Yatra)**, in which **Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra and Devi Subhadra** are publicly bathed with water from **108 sacred pots**.
- **About the Rath Yatra:** organised in the **Shukla Paksha of the lunar month of Ashadha (June–July)**. The three deities — **Jagannath, his brother Balabhadra and sister Subhadra** — are seated in **three huge wooden chariots** and taken to the **Gundicha Temple**.
- The journey starts from the **Jagannath Temple, Puri**, and Lord Jagannath's chariot is pulled up to **Gundicha (which is the house of the Lord's maternal aunt/uncle)**.
- **Note — Bahuda Yatra:** the return journey after staying **one month** at the Gundicha Temple is known as the **Bahuda Yatra**.
- **Note — Niladri Bije:** the symbol of the deities' return into the **Sanctum (Garbhagriha)**.

question: Who inaugurated the international seminar 'Saushrutam 2026'?
detail:
- **President Droupadi Murmu** inaugurated **'Saushrutam 2026'** at the **All India Institute of Ayurveda (AIIA), New Delhi**.
- The International Seminar was held from **15–17 July 2026** and was organised by the **Ministry of Ayush** on the occasion of **Sushruta Jayanti**.
- **Saushrutam 2026** is an international conference based on **Ayurveda**, in which Ayurveda experts, doctors, scientists and researchers from India and abroad take part.
- Its aim is to promote **Ayurvedic Surgery, Evidence-based Ayurveda**, and **Research and Institutional Collaboration** in the field, and to link Ayurveda with **Modern Science** to boost its research, treatment and global use.
- **About Maharshi Sushruta:** he is called the **"Father of Surgery"** and is regarded as the founder of ancient Indian surgery.
- His famous work is the **Sushruta Samhita**, an ancient Indian text on **surgery and medical science**, containing detailed descriptions of various **surgical techniques, instruments and treatment methods**.

question: India recently launched the 'Seafarer-First' Emergency Maritime Safety Plan for the safety of Indian seafarers operating in which region?
options: Persian Gulf & Strait of Hormuz Region
detail:
- The Government of India launched the **'Seafarer-First' Emergency Maritime Safety Plan** to protect Indian seafarers working in the **Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz and Gulf of Oman** region.
- The plan was launched after attacks on the merchant vessels **Motor Tanker Al Bahiyah** and **Mombasa** in the **Strait of Hormuz**.
- It will be implemented by the **Directorate General of Shipping (DGS)**.
- **About the Plan:** an emergency safety plan whose aim is to ensure the **safety, assistance and quick rescue** of Indian seafarers working at sea abroad.
- **Key Features:** **1. Real-time Tracking Dashboard** — live location and status monitoring of Indian seafarers working abroad; **2. 24×7 Helpline** — round-the-clock help for seafarers and their families; **3. Help from Indian embassies** in **Iran, Oman and UAE** whenever needed; **4. Threat Assessment before entering a High-Risk Area** — mandatory safety-risk assessment before sending a ship into a dangerous maritime zone; **5. Emergency Support** — immediate safety information, rescue and other necessary assistance during a crisis.
- **About DGS:** the principal body regulating **Merchant Shipping, Seafarer Welfare and Maritime Safety** in India. Under the **Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways**; headquarters **Mumbai, Maharashtra**.

question: The portable self-powered wearable device recently developed by Indian scientists is designed to detect which gas?
detail:
- Indian scientists have developed a **Portable, Self-powered and Wearable Ammonia (NH₃) Sensing Device**.
- The device was developed by scientists of the **Centre for Nano and Soft Matter Sciences (CeNS), Bengaluru**, an autonomous research institute under the **Department of Science and Technology (DST)**.
- **What is an Ammonia Sensing Device?** A sensor that detects the amount of **Ammonia (NH₃)** gas present in the air and gives a timely **warning or alert**.
- It can detect ammonia in quantities as low as **319 Parts Per Billion (ppb)**.
- It works at **Room Temperature** and can be used in wearable devices such as **Smart Bands, Smart Textiles and Smart Home Safety Systems**.
- **Objectives:** to protect people by detecting an **Ammonia gas leak** early, and to guard against toxic gas in **factories, hospitals, laboratories and the agriculture sector**.
- **About Ammonia (NH₃):** a **colourless gas** used in **Fertilizers, Refrigeration and Chemical Industries**. In large amounts it is harmful to the **eyes, skin and respiratory system**.

question: What is the total proposed power generation capacity of the Mahi Banswara Rajasthan Atomic Power Project (MBRAPP) to be set up in Banswara district of Rajasthan?
detail:
- The **Mahi Banswara Rajasthan Atomic Power Project (MBRAPP)** in **Banswara district, Rajasthan** will have a total proposed capacity of **2,800 MW**.
- Under the project, **4 indigenous Pressurised Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs)** of **700 MWe** capacity each will be installed.
- **Anushakti Vidhyut Nigam Limited (ASHVINI)** announced the invitation of **tenders** for the project.
- **About ASHVINI:** a **Joint Venture** company of the **Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL)** and the **National Thermal Power Corporation Limited (NTPC)**, formed to increase electricity generation from nuclear energy in India.
- **Equity:** **NPCIL – 51%, NTPC – 49%**.
- **Main work:** to **build, own and operate** Nuclear Power Plants.

question: When is International Justice Day celebrated every year?
detail:
- **International Justice Day** is celebrated every year on **17 July**.
- The day is also known as **International Criminal Justice Day** and the **World Day for International Justice**.
- It is observed to promote **international criminal justice** across the world.
- The day also commemorates the adoption of the **Rome Statute treaty on 17 July 1998**.
- The day was started in **2010** at the **Rome Statute conference held in Uganda**.
- **About the Rome Statute treaty:** it is an **international treaty**; the **International Criminal Court (ICC)** was established by this treaty. It was **signed on 17 July 1998** and **came into force on 1 July 2002**. **Members – 125**.

question: Which country topped the medal table at the inaugural Asian U-23 Athletics Championships 2026?
detail:
- **China** topped the medal table at the inaugural **Asian U-23 Athletics Championships 2026** with **23 gold medals**.
- The first (inaugural) edition was organised in **Ordos, China**.
- **India finished fourth**, winning a total of **16 medals — 3 gold, 4 silver and 9 bronze**.
- The competition was organised by **Asian Athletics (AA)**.
- **About Asian Athletics (AA):** the **Continental Governing Body** that runs **Athletics (Track & Field)** in Asia and organises various Athletics Championships. Former name — **Asian Athletics Association (AAA)**; founded **1973**; headquarters **Bangkok, Thailand**; President — **General Dahlan Jumaan Al Hamad**.

question: Recently, the Tripura Government signed an MoU with which company to develop an Artificial Intelligence (AI) Ecosystem?
detail:
- The **Tripura Government** signed a **Memorandum of Understanding (MoU)** with **Google India** to develop an **Artificial Intelligence (AI) Ecosystem**.
- The agreement was signed during the **Destination Tripura Business Conclave 2026** at **Hapania, Tripura**.
- Its aim is to modernise **AI, Cloud Infrastructure and Citizen Services**, promote **Digital Literacy and Skill Development**, and use Google's technical expertise in **Governance, Education and Entrepreneurship**.
- The initiative targets preparing about **5,000 technical graduates every year** as an **industry-ready workforce** through **AI and Digital Skills**.
- **About Google India:** the **Indian subsidiary** of Google, working in **Internet Services, Cloud Computing, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Digital Technologies**. Established **2004**; headquarters **Hyderabad, Telangana**.

question: What is the total budgetary outlay of the recently approved Semicon Mission 2.0?
detail:
- The **Union Cabinet** approved **Semicon Mission 2.0** with a budgetary outlay of **₹1.27 lakh crore**, along with the **Mobile Phone Manufacturing Scheme** with an outlay of **₹62,500 crore**.
- The aim of these schemes is to make India a **Global Hub of Semiconductor and Mobile Phone Manufacturing**, and to boost **domestic manufacturing, investment and employment**.
- Under it, the target is to attract investment of about **₹4 lakh crore** and achieve **Semiconductor Production worth about ₹2 lakh crore**.
- **About Semiconductors:** materials whose **electrical conductivity** lies between that of a **conductor and an insulator**. They are used to make **electronic chips**, which are used in **mobile phones, computers, automobiles, defence systems** and other electronic devices.
- **About the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM):** a national initiative of the Government of India started to develop a **Semiconductor Ecosystem** in the country and promote **Chip Design, Manufacturing (Fab), Packaging** and semiconductor investment. **Launched – 2021**; implemented by the **India Semiconductor Mission (ISM)**; **Ministry – Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY)**.
